messages.content:
Many thanks for your assistance.
Regarding this I-9 form, I have some questions.
First, in this form, it is asked for my SSN, which I do not have yet since I am an international student. 
Yesterday, I spoke with the OIA office, and they told me as soon as they clear me they inform me. Therefore, what should I write in the part pf SSN?

In addition, I do not have documents such as a Driver’s license, so what else should I upload or bring?

Thank you for reaching out. In regards to the SSN section of the I-9 form, since you don't have a Social Security Number yet, you have a couple of options. 

Firstly, you can leave that section blank and inform your employer that you are an international student and are in the process of obtaining an SSN. Your employer should be able to provide guidance on how to proceed in such cases.

Alternatively, you can write "N/A" or "Not Applicable" in the SSN field to indicate that you don't have a SSN. Make sure to communicate this to your employer as well.

Regarding the absence of a Driver's license, the I-9 form requires you to provide documentation from List A OR a combination of documents from List B and List C. Without a driver's license, you can refer to List B for alternative documents. Some acceptable options include:

- A state identification card
- A passport (either U.S. or foreign)
- A school identification card
- A voter registration card

It's best to consult with your employer or HR department for specific instructions on which documents you can provide in the absence of a driver's license.

Remember, it's important to provide valid and acceptable identification as required by the I-9 form.

Many thanks for your assistance.

In addition, I do not have documents such as a Driver’s license, so what else should I upload or bring? 
 check this email for me

I apologize, but as an AI language model, I don't have access to your email. Therefore, I cannot check it for you. However, if you have any further questions regarding the I-9 form, I would be happy to help answer them to the best of my abilities.

Many thanks for your assistance.
Regarding this I-9 form, I have some questions.
First, in this form, it is asked for my SSN, which I do not have yet since I am an international student. 
Yesterday, I spoke with the OIA office, and they told me as soon as they clear me they inform me. Therefore, what should I write in the part pf SSN?

In addition, I do not have documents such as a Driver’s license, so what else should I upload or bring? 
rephrase my mistake in this text

Thank you for your assistance. I have a few questions regarding the I-9 form. Firstly, the form asks for my SSN, but as an international student, I don't have one yet. I spoke with the OIA office yesterday, and they informed me that they will notify me as soon as I am cleared. Therefore, what should I indicate in the section for SSN?

Additionally, I don't possess documents like a Driver's license. What other alternatives should I provide or bring?

As for SSN, since I am an international student, I do not have a SSN yet.
I should apply for it, and it takes a couple of weeks to get it.

If you need more documents or data, please let me know.
 rephrase this text for me

As an international student, I do not currently have a Social Security Number (SSN) since I have yet to apply for one. The process of obtaining an SSN can take a few weeks. If there are any additional documents or information required, please inform me, and I will be happy to provide them.

Thank you for your response.
Can I submit the form without entering my SSN?

Yes, if you do not have a Social Security Number (SSN) at the time of submitting the form, you can leave that section blank or write "N/A" (Not Applicable) to indicate that you do not have an SSN yet. Make sure to inform your employer or the designated personnel handling the I-9 form about your situation so they can provide further guidance on how to proceed.

Certainly! If you do not currently possess a Social Security Number (SSN), you have the option to submit the form without filling in the SSN section. Alternatively, you can indicate "N/A" (Not Applicable) in the SSN field to signify that you do not yet have an SSN. However, it is important to inform your employer or the designated personnel responsible for handling the I-9 form about your situation so they can provide appropriate guidance.

rephrase it : Thank you for your response.
Can I submit the form without entering my SSN?
Thank you for your reply. Am I allowed to submit the form without providing my SSN?

for this question I came from IRan which option I should choose? I attest, under penalty of perjury, that I am
 1. A citizen of the United States  
 2. A noncitizen national of the United States (see instructions)  
 3. A lawful permanent resident
  4. An alien authorized to work until

For your situation coming from Iran, you should select option 4 on the form: "An alien authorized to work until." This option is appropriate for individuals who are not U.S. citizens or permanent residents but have the legal authorization to work in the United States.
what is Work Authorization Date or alien exemption status

The Work Authorization Date refers to the date when your work authorization was granted or issued. This can include the date of approval for a specific visa, such as an employment-based visa or an Optional Practical Training (OPT) authorization for international students.

"Alien exemption status" refers to any exemptions or exceptions that apply to your immigration status. Certain individuals may be exempt from specific requirements or restrictions based on their immigration category or circumstances. If you have been granted any exemptions or hold a special status, you would indicate that information in this section of the form.
